metadata.summary: B2B advertising on LinkedIn by job title, seniority, company, and ABM. CPCs $5-15; minimum $50/day budget. Lead Gen Forms parallel with Website Conversions. Refresh every 14-21 days.

content.title: LinkedIn Ads
content.summary: B2B advertising on LinkedIn using job title, seniority, company size, and ABM targeting. CPCs run $5-15; minimum $50/day budget is required for the optimizer to exit the learning phase. Use Lead Gen Forms in parallel with Website Conversions — LGF has higher volume but lower intent. Refresh creative every 14-21 days. Never increase budget more than 25% in one step without human approval.

why.p: LinkedIn is the only ad platform with reliable professional identity targeting (title, seniority, company). This precision makes it the correct channel for reaching narrow B2B ICPs — VPs at fintech, Directors at mid-market SaaS — that are unreachable at equivalent cost on Meta or Google. The tradeoff is cost: CPM of $30-80 and CPC of $5-15 require AOV or LTV above ~$1k to be viable.

list.item: B2B campaigns where the buyer is identifiable by job title, seniority, or company (ABM)
Lead-gen forms with LinkedIn-prefilled fields (email, company, title) for high-quality MQLs
Reaching narrow ICPs unreachable on Meta or Google at acceptable CPL
Retargeting site visitors with the LinkedIn Insight Tag for warm-funnel B2B nurture
B2C, low-AOV products, or impulse purchases — CPC of $5-15 destroys unit economics below ~$1k LTV
Daily budgets under $50 — LinkedIn's optimizer cannot exit learning phase
Audiences under 20k — costs spike, learning never converges, frequency fatigue triggers early
Pure brand awareness on a tight budget — Meta/YouTube cost-per-impression is 5-10x cheaper

section.name: campaign-objectives
section.p: Objective selection determines the optimization signal and which metrics are prioritized in the campaign.

list.item: Brand Awareness — top of funnel, impressions
Website Visits — traffic, clicks
Engagement — reactions and comments on content
Video Views — video content views
Lead Generation — native lead gen forms
Website Conversions — signups, demo requests, purchases

rule.statement: Default to Lead Generation or Website Conversions for B2B campaigns; use Brand Awareness only when ICP audience size exceeds 50,000.
rule.rationale: B2B budget is too expensive for pure awareness goals below $1k LTV. Conversion-optimized objectives produce measurable pipeline outcomes from day one.

section.name: targeting-strategies
section.p: LinkedIn's professional targeting is its core strength. Build audiences in distinct layers to enable independent performance diagnosis and scaling decisions.

rule.statement: Build audiences in three layers — ABM list (named accounts) + ICP (title + size + industry) + similar audience expansion — run as separate campaigns, not one targeting blob.
rule.rationale: Mixing audiences in a single campaign prevents performance diagnosis. A separate campaign per layer reveals which audience converts at what CPL and lets you scale or cut independently.

list.item: Job Title: exact titles or title functions — primary B2B signal
Job Seniority: entry to C-level
Company Size: 1-10 to 10,000+
Company Name: specific accounts for ABM (upload CSV)
Industry: 140+ categories
Skills: professional skills
Groups: LinkedIn groups membership

rule.statement: Minimum audience size for conversion campaigns: 20,000. Minimum for awareness: 50,000. Below 20,000, learning never converges and CPL spikes.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn's optimizer needs a large enough pool to find efficient buyers. Narrow audiences cause excessive frequency, high CPMs, and no statistical learning signal.

section.name: ad-formats
section.p: LinkedIn supports multiple ad formats optimized for different messaging objectives and audience sizes.

list.item: Single Image: 1200x627 — standard promotion, most common
Carousel: 3-10 cards — multiple products or features
Video: 3-30 seconds — storytelling, demos
Text Ads: text only — low-budget awareness
Message Ads: Sponsored InMail — direct outreach
Lead Gen Forms: native forms pre-filled with LinkedIn data

rule.statement: Use Lead Gen Forms in parallel with Website Conversions; LGF has higher volume but lower intent — track both to revenue, not just CPL.
rule.rationale: Lead Gen Forms convert at higher rates due to pre-filled fields, but leads show lower purchase intent than those who visited the landing page. Both signals are needed to understand pipeline quality.

section.name: b2b-ad-copy-formula
section.p: LinkedIn CPCs of $5-15 only justify budget when copy speaks precisely to the ICP's role-specific problem. Generic messaging produces low CTR and wastes the targeting advantage.

rule.statement: Structure LinkedIn ad copy as: intro (problem or question for the role), headline (solution or value prop), CTA (clear action); never use generic copy that could target any professional.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn CPCs of $5-15 only justify budget when the copy speaks precisely to the ICP's role-specific problem. Generic copy produces low CTR (under 0.3%) and wastes the targeting advantage.

list.item: Intro text: 150 chars shown — hook with role-specific problem or question
Headline: 70 chars — clear value proposition
Description: 100 chars — supporting detail or proof point
CTA options: Learn More, Download, Sign Up, Request Demo
Image: 1200x627, professional quality

section.name: budget-and-cost-expectations
section.p: LinkedIn's cost structure is significantly higher than Meta but justified by professional targeting precision.

list.item: CPM: $30-80 typical range
CPC: $5-15 typical range
Cost per lead (LGF): $30-100+
Minimum daily budget: $50
Minimum test budget: $2,000-5,000 (enough data for optimization)

rule.statement: Do not run LinkedIn campaigns with a daily budget under $50; the optimizer cannot exit the learning phase and burns budget on noise.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn's algorithm requires a minimum number of daily actions (clicks or leads) to optimize. Below $50/day, the optimizer never achieves statistical significance within a reasonable window.

section.name: bidding-progression
section.p: Campaign bidding strategy should evolve as conversion data accumulates and the optimization algorithm learns.

rule.statement: Bid manually (CPC) for the first 14 days of any new campaign until 50+ form fills accumulate; then switch to Maximum Delivery for the optimizer to use the data.
rule.rationale: Maximum Delivery needs conversion history to optimize efficiently. Without data, it makes random bidding decisions that waste the first weeks of spend.

list.item: Weeks 1-2: Manual CPC — full bid control, data collection
After 50+ form fills: Maximum Delivery (automatic)
Cost cap: set target CPA after sufficient data — constrains optimizer

section.name: optimization
section.p: Ongoing creative refresh and performance tuning are essential for sustained ROI on LinkedIn campaigns.

rule.statement: Refresh creative every 14-21 days on LinkedIn; CTR decay is faster than Meta because the feed is sparser and users see the same ads more quickly.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn's professional audience is smaller and more targeted than Meta's. High frequency against a small ICP list leads to ad fatigue within 2-3 weeks.

list.item: CTR benchmark: 0.4% good, 0.8%+ great
Lead form rate: 10% good, 15%+ great
CPL target: <$75 good, <$50 great
Lead quality: track MQL rate and cost per opportunity, not just CPL

rule.statement: Exclude existing customers and converted leads via uploaded customer-list audiences; LinkedIn has no native exclude-converters toggle.
rule.rationale: Without exclusions, budget is wasted showing acquisition ads to existing customers, inflating lead counts with people who cannot convert again.

section.name: api-versioning
section.p: LinkedIn's API versioning scheme requires active maintenance to prevent deprecation errors.

rule.statement: Always include the LinkedIn-Version header in every API call and refresh the pinned version within 6 months; outdated versions return 410 deprecation errors.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn changes its API versioning monthly. Agents pinning a version over 6 months old will hit 410 responses without a clear migration path in the error message.

rule.statement: Refresh OAuth access tokens before they expire (60-day lifetime); implement a token refresh flow that runs proactively, not reactively after a 401.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn access tokens expire in 60 days. Agents relying on 401 errors to trigger refresh fail silently at midnight when the token expires mid-run, losing that day's data.

section.name: cost-units
section.p: Cost measurement on LinkedIn uses a different unit system than other ad platforms, creating a common conversion bug.

rule.statement: LinkedIn cost data is in costInLocalCurrency as a decimal float (e.g., 45.50 USD), NOT in micros like Google Ads. Do not divide by 1,000,000.
rule.rationale: Agents porting Google Ads micros logic to LinkedIn will divide costs by 1,000,000 and report CPLs of $0.000065 instead of $65 — undetectable if alerts are set in micros units.

section.name: rate-limits-and-quotas
section.p: API quota management is critical for multi-account or multi-app scenarios.

rule.statement: Rate limits apply per app, not per account; multi-tenant agents must implement a per-app token bucket and back off on 429 responses.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn enforces rate limits at the OAuth app level, not per ad account. An agent managing 10 accounts with the same app will hit the limit 10x faster than a single-account agent.

antipattern.description: Assuming Lead Gen Form field order is stable across form versions.
antipattern.reason: Custom question fields are auto-keyed (e.g., question_1ab23). Field order and keys change if the form is edited. Agents must cache the field-map per form version, never assume index positions.

section.name: attribution-lag
section.p: LinkedIn's reporting lag and multi-touch attribution window require patience and discipline in campaign evaluation.

rule.statement: Wait 24-48 hours for LinkedIn metrics to stabilize before evaluating campaign performance; do not retune based on same-day data.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn reporting has a 24-48 hour lag before conversion numbers are stable. Early optimization based on incomplete data causes over-reaction to noise.

rule.statement: Insight Tag conversions backfill for up to 90 days; do not declare a campaign a loser before the lookback window closes.
rule.rationale: View-through conversions and delayed form completions backfill over 90 days. Cutting a campaign at 30 days permanently loses attribution for late-converting leads.

section.name: human-in-loop-checkpoints
section.p: Even with full automation capability, these decision points require human judgment or explicit approval.

list.item: Budget increase above 25% in one step: human approval required
Final ad copy approval before launch: human required
ABM target-account list sign-off: human required
Changing campaign objective: human required

template.purpose: Pre-launch checklist: targeting, creative, budget, lead gen form
B2B ad copy template: intro, headline, description, CTA
Daily spend pacing check against daily budget cap
